title: Media Reference v1
description: The auditable, NON-LEAKING media-provenance block (EPIC-CORE-013) carried by a transcript capture — in the L0 raw note frontmatter (`media:`) and the capture event payload. It records the transcriber's content hash (`media_id`), the sha256 of the captured transcript text (`transcript_sha256`, for strict idempotency + conflict detection), the classification of the original-media pointer (`ref_class` — WHY it was treated public/private), and exactly one handle: a non-sensitive public `ref`, or a `secref_…` id whose opaque locator lives only inside the secure_ref pointer file. By construction (additionalProperties:false) there is NO field for a raw URL/path/locator, so a private locator can never be smuggled into a note or event.

media_id.description: The transcriber's content hash for the source media (stable across rename). Opaque, non-secret; the idempotency/conflict key.

transcript_sha256.description: Lowercase hex sha256 of the captured transcript text. Re-ingesting the same media_id with a different value is a media_id_conflict.

media_ref_fp.description: Optional one-way sha256 of the original-media pointer (conflict comparator). Non-reversible — carries no locator value, but detects a changed media reference exactly across re-ingests, including private/secref pointers.

ref_class.description: Classification of the original-media pointer. Only the class is stored — NEVER the raw URL/path/locator value.

ref.description: Present iff ref_class is public_ref: a non-sensitive external pointer (e.g. a public URL or a non-private path). Metadata only.

secref.description: Present iff ref_class is NOT public_ref: a secure_ref id. The opaque locator lives only inside secure_refs/<id>.md; it never appears here.
